title: WordPress oEmbed Controller Flow

class_info.name: WP_oEmbed_Controller
class_info.purpose: oEmbed API endpoint controller — registers REST API routes and delivers oEmbed response data

activities.name: register_routes
steps: Register REST route: GET oembed/1.0/embed — callback=get_item, permission=__return_true, args=[url(required,uri), format(default:json, sanitize:wp_oembed_ensure_format), maxwidth(default:$maxwidth, sanitize:absint)]

Register REST route: GET oembed/1.0/proxy — callback=get_proxy_item, permission=get_proxy_item_permissions_check, args=[url(required,uri), format(enum:json|xml,default:json), maxwidth(integer,default:$maxwidth,sanitize:absint), maxheight(integer,sanitize:absint), discover(boolean,default:true)]

activities.name: get_proxy_item
steps: Unset $args['_wpnonce'] (security token removed before cache key)

key_conditions: get_proxy_item: local URL match via get_oembed_response_data_for_url → early return
get_proxy_item: external fetch fails → try classic embed handler, then 404
external_dependencies: url_to_postid — resolves URL to post ID
get_oembed_response_data — builds oEmbed data for a local post
_wp_oembed_get_object() — returns WP_oEmbed singleton; calls get_data() and data2html()
$wp_scripts — global script queue for enqueued scripts in embed handler response
routes_registered: GET /wp-json/oembed/1.0/embed  — public, returns local post oEmbed data
GET /wp-json/oembed/1.0/proxy  — requires edit_posts capability, proxies external oEmbed
important_notes: '/proxy' route requires 'edit_posts' capability (editor role or higher).
Proxy response is cached via WordPress transients; default TTL = DAY_IN_SECONDS, filterable via 'rest_oembed_ttl'.
Proxy has three-tier fetch: (1) local short-circuit, (2) external WP_oEmbed fetch, (3) classic embed handler fallback.
maxwidth/maxheight params are remapped to width/height for WP_oEmbed::get_data() compatibility.
_wpnonce is stripped before cache key generation to ensure cache-key stability across nonce rotations.
